intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. P1 (Yellow Pot) is empty of visible foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows a single, isolated sprout. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but lacks the primary Pothos specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Cluster: Contains a single, thin, green sprout. No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp appearance; no visible fungal growth or surface debris.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: One solitary, uncatalogued sprout in the black pot cluster.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Total absence of the registered botanical specimens across all pots.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, the biome has transitioned from a state of suspected dormancy to confirmed systemic loss. The single sprout in the black pot has remained static in position, showing no significant growth or leaf development over the 5-day observation window.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is effectively non-viable. The lack of foliage in all pots suggests either total senescence or complete removal of the primary specimens. The single sprout is the only biological activity, but it is insufficient to constitute a healthy biome.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, unidentified sprout in a pot otherwise devoid of its registered occupant.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the registered botanical collection. The pots are largely barren, with the exception of a single, unidentified sprout in the black pot cluster. The white rabbit anchor remains in P3, but the Pothos is entirely missing. The environment appears stable but biologically depleted.
